title: Delicious Smoky Korean Noodles Kalguksu
description: A smoky and velvety Korean dish from the Gyeongsang region, featuring bay leaves and served in a stone bowl, perfect for curing hangovers. This recipe is dash-friendly and served in the form of kalguksu.

ingredients.name: pork bones
ingredients.notes: you can also use beef bones

instructions: In a large pot, combine pork bones, bay leaves, garlic, and ginger, and pour in enough cold water to cover the ingredients.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 1 hour.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add the kalguksu noodles to the pot and cook according to the package instructions.
While the noodles are cooking, heat the sesame oil in a small pan over medium heat.
Add the chopped green onions to the pan and cook until they are slightly caramelized.
To serve, place the cooked noodles in a stone bowl, then ladle the hot broth over the noodles.
Garnish with the caramelized green onions, a sprinkle of salt, and a few grinds of black pepper.
Drizzle with soy sauce to taste, then serve immediately.
